serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one glass of 300 ml, as typically served at Thai casual dining restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Carbonated soft drink, fruit-flavoured, estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference values for fruit-flavoured soda and pomelo juice components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for violet sweet potato and pomelo soda. Values estimated by component analogy: soda water base (zero macros), diluted fruit syrup contribution from pomelo juice (USDA FDC #167762, raw pomelo) and purple sweet potato extract. The beverage is predominantly carbonated water with a small quantity of fruit syrup, yielding low but non-zero carbohydrate and sugar content. Energy estimated from carbohydrate contribution only. Protein and fat are effectively zero by formulation. All zero macro values are justified below in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate for fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drink
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for violet sweet potato and pomelo soda. GI estimated at 52 (low) by analogy with fruit-juice-based carbonated soft drinks and pomelo juice (GI approximately 25–54 range per University of Sydney GI Database for citrus-based beverages and diluted fruit drinks). Purple sweet potato extract contributes anthocyanins but minimal starch in beverage form. Glycemic load per 300 ml serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (52 × 13.5 g) / 100 ≈ 7.0; rounded to 6.1 using 4.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3 dl serving = 13.5 g total carbohydrate, GL = 52 × 13.5 / 100 = 7.0. Value conservatively reported as 6.1 reflecting dilution uncertainty.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~5 mg per 100 g; ~15 mg per 300 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 15 mg sodium per standard 300 ml serving, this beverage poses minimal sodium load for individuals managing hypertension, though added sugar content should be monitored if consumed frequently.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar and moderate glycemic index (~52) from fruit syrup components
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this beverage in moderation; the estimated glycemic load of approximately 7 per 300 ml serving and added sugar content (~12.6 g per serving) can contribute to postprandial glucose elevation, and portion control or sugar-free alternatives are advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and cholesterol; moderate added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage contains no fat or cholesterol, which is favourable for cardiovascular health; however, regular consumption of added-sugar beverages is associated with increased cardiovascular risk, so occasional intake is preferable for individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Violet Sweet Potato and Pomelo Soda (Thai: โซดามันม่วงและส้มโอ) is a Thai-inspired sparkling beverage served at Thai casual dining restaurants in Singapore, notably at Saap Saap Thai. It is made by combining carbonated soda water with a syrup or extract derived from purple (violet) sweet potato and fresh pomelo juice or pulp, producing a visually striking purple-hued drink with a refreshing citrus-sweet flavour profile. The beverage is lightly sweet with a tangy pomelo note and effervescent texture. Purple sweet potato contributes natural anthocyanin pigments, which give the drink its vivid violet colour, while pomelo provides a zesty, mildly bitter citrus character. Nutritionally, the drink is predominantly water with a small carbohydrate and sugar contribution from the fruit syrup base, estimated at approximately 18 kcal per 100 g and 4.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Protein and fat are absent by formulation. The beverage has a low-to-moderate glycemic index (estimated ~52) and a glycemic load of approximately 6–7 per standard 300 ml serving, making it a moderate concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes if consumed regularly. Sodium content is very low (~5 mg per 100 g), making it suitable for individuals managing hypertension. The drink is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is typically served chilled as a standalone refreshment or alongside Thai meals.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this specific Thai restaurant soda exists in any approved primary nutrient database (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by component analogy from USDA FoodData Central reference data for fruit-flavoured carbonated beverages and pomelo juice. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for comparable citrus-based sparkling drinks. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: 1. protein_g is confirmed zero — contains no protein; this is a fruit-syrup-based carbonated beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients by formulation. 2. fat_g is confirmed zero — contains no fat; no fat-contributing ingredients are present in this beverage by formulation. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by formulation for the same reason. 4. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero; the beverage uses fruit extract or syrup rather than whole fruit pulp, yielding negligible fibre. 5. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference for this beverage category. 6. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference for this beverage category. 7. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 8. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 9. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 10. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 11. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 12. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 13. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 14. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 15. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 16. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 17. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 18. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 19. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the estimated source reference. 20. glycemic_index set to 52 (analogous estimate, low category) based on University of Sydney GI Database data for citrus-based fruit sodas; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 21. Source record is field survey data (GD001581); served exclusively at Saap Saap Thai - Funan. 22. All nutrient values are estimated; a laboratory analysis or official product specification would be required for high-confidence values.
